entries.definition:
innocence], that they should imprison him for a while.
مسجونين masjūnīn [pl. of pas. part. masjūn] one imprisoned
(26:29) لئن اتخذت إلها غيري لأجعلنك من المسجونين if you take any god
other than me, I will make you among the prisoners.

سجين sijjīn [n.] a written record enumerating the bad deeds of
the wicked (83:7–9) إن كتاب الفجار لفي سجين وما أدراك ما سجين كتاب مرقوم
the list of the wicked is in Sijjīn, what will explain to you what
Sijjīn is?–a clearly numbered list.

entries.definition:
s–j–y (of the night) to become silent, quiet or still, to calm
down, to become tranquil, placid. Of this root, only سجى sajā
occurs once in the Qur’an.

سجى sajā u [v. intrans.] to be quiet, to be still, to be tranquil, to
be serene (93:2) والليل إذا سجى and by the night, when it grows still.
